mnemonics.title: S.N.F.S. Memory Line
mnemonics.content: 'Semantic Networks Form Structured Knowledge' - Each word reminds you: Semantic (meaning), Networks (graph connections), Form (frames as templates), Structured (scripts as sequences), Knowledge (representation)

mnemonics.title: ISA-AKO-PART Remember
mnemonics.content: ISA (Is-A) = inheritance relationship; AKO (A-Kind-Of) = category membership; PART-OF = component relationship. Remember: 'I Always Keep Organized Papers' for ISA, AKO, PART-OF

mnemonics.title: FRAME Formula
mnemonics.content: F-R-A-M-E = Facts + Relations + Attributes + Methods + Exceptions - a frame contains slots (attributes) with fillers (values) and default values for inheritance

mnemonics.title: SCRIPT Steps
mnemonics.content: S-C-R-I-P-T = Scene + Characters + Roles + Inferences + Props + Transitions - a script organizes stereotyped event sequences with entry conditions and results

acronyms.usage: Represents inheritance hierarchy. Use when a node is a subclass/superclass relationship (e.g., 'Robin ISA Bird').
Used in semantic nets to show category membership. Similar to ISA but emphasizes type relationship.
Represents attribute or composition relationship (e.g., 'Car HAS-A engine').
Represents compositional relationship showing component structure (e.g., 'Wheel PART-OF Car').
A knowledge representation system using semantic nets. Remember as the 'SN' in SNePS = Semantic Net.
A semantic net representation focusing on essential node-link structures.

visualTips.visualization: Picture a spider web or mind map where each junction (node) is a concept (Bird, Robin) and each strand (link) is a relationship (ISA, HAS-A). Darker strands = stronger relationships. Use arrows to show direction of inheritance.

Imagine a filing cabinet drawer labeled 'Animal' containing a form template with blank slots: [Name:], [Color:], [Legs:], [Can Fly?:]. Fill slots for specific animals. Nested drawers show frame inheritance.

Think of a movie script format: Scene 1 (Restaurant), Characters (Customer, Waiter), Props (Menu, Food, Bill), Actions (Sit, Order, Eat, Pay). Arrows show sequence flow. Entry conditions = 'Hungry + Has Money', Results = 'Full + No Money'.
Draw an inverted family tree where properties flow downward from parent to child nodes. Use color coding: red for inherited properties, blue for own properties. Dotted lines for default values.

analogies.analogy: Like a social network graph - people are nodes, friendships/relationships are links. Your friend's friend inherits some connection through you, just like properties inherit down the network.

Like a job application form or HTML form template. The form (frame) has fields (slots) with labels like Name, Address, Experience. You fill in (instantiate) with specific values. Default values are like pre-checked boxes.

Like a restaurant script or a recipe. You know the typical sequence: enter → sit → order → eat → pay → leave. Each restaurant follows this script. Props (utensils, menu) and roles (waiter, chef) are consistent.

Like a dictionary entry. The headword (slot name) has definitions (fillers). Some words have multiple meanings (multiple fillers). Default values are like standard definitions before checking the specific context.

Frames are like classes (templates), instances are like objects. ISA links = inheritance. Slots = attributes/methods. Default values = default parameters. This connects AI concepts to programming knowledge.
